articles.title: Late-add petitions after the deadline
articles.summary: How and when an advisor can submit a late-add petition on behalf of a student.
policy: Petitions accepted within 5 weeks of the term start.
Requires instructor approval (email is sufficient).
Requires a documented, qualifying reason (medical, advising error, military).
Submitted via PeopleSoft > Records > Petition Workflow.
articles.limits: Hard cutoff at week 6 — no petitions accepted after.

articles.title: Late-drop petitions and 'W' grades
articles.summary: Process for late drops and how 'W' grades are recorded.
policy: Standard drop deadline is end of week 7.
Late drop requires petition with documented hardship.
If approved, transcript shows 'W' (no GPA impact).
Multiple late drops (3+) trigger an academic standing review.
articles.limits: No late drops in summer sessions shorter than 5 weeks.

articles.title: FERPA and parent inquiries
articles.summary: How to handle parent or third-party requests for student information.
policy: Never disclose student records to parents without a signed FERPA release on file.
A FERPA release can be added via MyASU > Profile > Information Release.
Even with a release, prefer to redirect to the student.
Health/safety emergency exception requires a Dean-of-Students consult.

articles.title: Documentation standards for advising notes
articles.summary: What must (and must not) go into Salesforce Advising notes.
policy: Notes must be objective, concise, action-oriented.
No diagnosis, opinion, or hearsay.
Tag the appointment type and any referrals made.
Notes are FERPA records — assume the student will read them.
articles.limits: Edits older than 24 hours require manager approval.

articles.title: Academic standing — probation and dismissal
articles.summary: Triggers and timelines for probation, continued probation, and dismissal.
policy: Probation = cumulative GPA < 2.00 at end of term.
Continued probation if next-term GPA ≥ 2.00 but cumulative still < 2.00.
Dismissal recommended after 2 consecutive terms below 2.00 cumulative.
All status changes require the standardized intervention plan (form ADV-007).
articles.limits: Dismissal is final after college dean's signature; appeals go to the standing committee.
